eventMetadata.context_description: Ilia Topuria holds the UFC lightweight title after a first-round knockout of Charles Oliveira in June 2025 and enters 2026 as the clear frontrunner, with traders pricing his retention through year-end at 70% based on his finishing power, undefeated record, and recent two-division success. Arman Tsarukyan sits at 20.7% as the consensus next challenger following his November submission win over Dan Hooker and consistent ranking as the top contender, though his path remains blocked by Topuria’s planned April-June return. Justin Gaethje’s 12.4% reflects his interim-title victory over Paddy Pimblett in January 2026 and veteran experience, while Charles Oliveira at 7.5% and Benoît Saint Denis at 3.0% trail due to recent setbacks and thinner title windows. The market reflects Topuria’s strong recent form against established lightweights amid an otherwise unsettled division.

markets.question: Game Handicap: CNV (-2.5) vs CCG Esports (+2.5)
markets.description:
This market refers to the LoL Upper bracket final match between CCG Esports and Conviction in the North American Challengers League Playoffs, initially scheduled for May 28 at 4:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to "Conviction" if Conviction wins 3 or more games than CCG Esports in this match.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "CCG Esports". 

Games won by forfeit, disqualification, walkover, or default are counted towards the handicap, provided that the match is completed.

If the match is canceled (not played at all for any reason), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's match forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends due to the clinching game being forfeited this will count as a completed match.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

events.title: LoL: CCG Esports vs Conviction (BO5) - North American Challengers League Playoffs
events.description:
This market refers to the LoL Upper bracket final match between CCG Esports and Conviction in the North American Challengers League Playoffs, initially scheduled for May 28 at 4:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to "CCG Esports" if CCG Esports win the match against Conviction.

This market will resolve to "Conviction" if Conviction win the match against CCG Esports.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Conviction enters this North American Challengers League Playoffs best-of-five as the clear historical favorite against CCG Esports, holding a 4-0 edge in recent head-to-head series across 2025 and 2026 splits. Conviction has posted consistent regular-season results, including a 2-0 sweep of CCG in April 2026, while demonstrating stronger roster stability and execution in high-stakes matches. CCG Esports has shown occasional resilience in individual games but has struggled to close out series against Conviction. The double-elimination bracket context adds playoff implications for both teams advancing toward potential promotion paths or summer split positioning. No major roster changes or injury reports have surfaced ahead of the May 29 matchup.

events.title: Super Rugby Pacific: Western Force vs Waratahs
events.description: This event is for the upcoming Super Rugby Pacific match between Western Force and Waratahs, scheduled for May 30 2026.

eventMetadata.context_description: Western Force enter the Super Rugby Pacific rematch as clear favorites due to superior recent form and home advantage at their Perth base. The side has secured four wins in their last six outings, including a gritty 20-17 upset victory over the Waratahs in Sydney on May 1 that highlighted improved forward discipline and attacking structure. In contrast, the Waratahs have endured inconsistent results and multiple key injuries to players such as Joey Walton and Matt Philip, contributing to their lower ladder position and diminished momentum. Traders appear to price in the Force's momentum and familiar conditions as decisive edges, while acknowledging the Waratahs' potential for a response in a tightly contested Australian derby.

markets.question: Will Meta (META) finish week of May 25 above $560?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the official closing price for Meta Platforms, Inc. (META) on the final day of trading of the specified week (normally Friday) is higher than the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

If the final session is shortened (for example, due to a market-holiday schedule), the official closing price published for that shortened session will still be used for resolution.

If no official closing price is published for that session (for example, due to a trading halt into the close, system issue, delisting, or other disruption), the market will use the last valid on-exchange trade price of the regular session as the effective closing price.

The resolution source for this market is Yahoo Finance, specifically the Meta Platforms, Inc. (META) "Close" prices available at https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/META/history, published under "Historical Prices."

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Yahoo Finance.

markets.question: US strike on Colombia by December 31?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if a US-initiated drone, missile, or air strike on the soil of Colombia is announced or credibly reported to have occurred by the listed date ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market, a qualifying "strike" is defined as the use of aerial bombs, drones, or missiles (including FPV and ATGM strikes as well as cruise or ballistic missiles) launched by any United States operatives, including military forces, intelligence agencies, or other U.S. government operatives, that physically impact ground territory within the listed country.

A strike on any area within the terrestrial territory (including rivers, lakes, ports, but excluding territorial sea) of the listed country counts.

Missiles or drones that are intercepted and surface-to-air missile strikes will not be sufficient for a "Yes" resolution, regardless of whether they land territory or cause damage.

Actions such as artillery fire, small arms fire, ground incursions, naval shelling, or cyberattacks will not qualify.

Any strike occurring during this market’s timeframe that is claimed by either Donald Trump or the U.S. government will qualify.

The primary resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

This market will remain open until the end of the second day after the resolution time. If the date/time of a qualifying strike cannot be confirmed by a consensus of credible reporting by that time, it will resolve to "No" regardless of whether a strike was later confirmed to have taken place.

eventMetadata.context_description: Tensions between the United States and Colombia have risen amid the Trump administration’s expanded strikes on suspected drug-smuggling vessels in the eastern Pacific and Caribbean, which Colombian officials link to routes originating in their country. President Trump’s public remarks suggesting military operations against Colombian cartels, following U.S. actions in Venezuela, prompted Colombian President Gustavo Petro to describe a “real threat” to sovereignty and warn of potential military response. Bilateral relations have featured both friction—including sanctions on Petro and suspended intelligence sharing—and cooperation, highlighted by a February 2026 White House meeting where the leaders agreed on joint targeting of narco figures, after which Colombian forces conducted strikes on guerrillas near the Venezuela border. No U.S. strikes on Colombian soil have occurred, and traders appear to weigh the balance between aggressive counter-narcotics policy and the strategic value of maintaining partnership with a key regional ally ahead of Colombia’s 2026 elections.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the KS-03 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the KS-03 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Democratic Representative Sharice Davids is seeking reelection in Kansas’s 3rd congressional district, which carries a Cook Partisan Voter Index of D+2 and has been rated Solid Democratic by the Cook Political Report and Inside Elections. Davids won the seat in 2024 by double digits and recently confirmed her 2026 candidacy, bolstering expectations of another victory. Multiple Republican primary candidates have emerged, but the district’s suburban Kansas City electorate and historical voting patterns continue to favor the Democratic nominee ahead of the August 2026 primaries and November general election. Trader consensus reflects these structural and incumbency advantages.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Ankara be 15°C or below on May 31?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Esenboğa Intl Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 31 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day for the Esenboğa Intl Airport Station, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/tr/%C3%A7ubuk/LTAC.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve until the first data point for the following date has been published on the resolution source.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Revisions to temperatures recorded within this market's timeframe will be considered until the first datapoint for the following date has been published, after which any alterations will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent numerical weather prediction models from agencies like the European Centre for Medium-Range Weather Forecasts indicate Ankara’s May 31 daytime maximum will likely peak near 20–21°C, aligning with the market’s tight clustering around those outcomes. Springtime warming over central Anatolia, driven by increasing solar insolation and weakening high-pressure influence, supports this range, while variable low-level moisture and possible weak frontal passages introduce modest uncertainty in the exact peak. Historical May climatology places average highs near 22°C, yet late-month variability from transient troughs can shift readings by 2–3°C. Updated model runs and observational data from Turkish meteorological stations over the next 48 hours will refine resolution criteria tied to official maximum temperature reports.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent forecasts from the India Meteorological Department and global models indicate a cooling influence over Lucknow from scattered thundershowers and increased cloud cover expected around May 31, pulling maximum temperatures down from recent 40–43°C readings. This setup aligns with pre-monsoon western disturbance activity that often suppresses daytime heating across the Indo-Gangetic plain in late May. The 91% market-implied probability for 35°C or below reflects strong trader consensus on these model outputs and observed trends, consistent with historical cases where similar moisture incursions cap highs near or below seasonal normals. markets.question: Will Sadegh Mahsouli be head of state in Iran end of 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the individual who de facto holds and exercises the powers of the head of state of the Islamic Republic of Iran on December 31, 2026 at 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, “de facto holds” refers to the individual who exercises primary governing authority over the Iranian state at that time, including effective control over the armed forces, national institutions, and core executive decision-making, regardless of formal title, constitutional designation, or international recognition.

Formal appointment, recognition by the United Nations, or recognition by foreign governments is not required.

If more than one individual claims to be head of state, this market will resolve to the individual who demonstrably exercises primary governing control within Iran’s territory at the specified time.

Indicators of de facto authority may include control over the armed forces and security services; control over executive ministries and state administrative institutions; enforcement of national laws; issuance of binding national directives; and effective control over the capital and core state infrastructure.

Symbolic status, foreign recognition without domestic control, nomination without effective authority, or expired prior service will not qualify.

If no individual exercises effective governing control at the specified time, this market will resolve to “No Head of State”.

The primary resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting assessing who exercises effective governing authority at the specified time.

markets.question: Will the People’s Bank of China increase rates in May?

An “increase” refers to any change in the 7-day reverse repo rate to a level higher than the most recent effective 7-day reverse repo rate.

A “decrease” refers to any change in the 7-day reverse repo rate to a level lower than the most recent effective 7-day reverse repo rate.

An official announcement of a change to the PBoC 7-day Reverse Repo Rate within this market’s timeframe will be sufficient to resolve this market, regardless of when the rate change is stated to go into effect.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the People’s Bank of China, including PBoC Open Market Operations announcements (https://www.pbc.gov.cn/en/3688110/3688181/index.html); however, a consensus of credible reporting on a change to the 7-day reverse repo rate may also be used.

markets.question: Will Microsoft have the best Math AI model at the end of June 2026?

markets.question: Will the lowest temperature in Seoul be 13°C on May 30?

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the lowest temperature recorded for all times on this day for the Incheon Intl Airport Station, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/kr/incheon/RKSI.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Revisions to temperatures recorded within this market's timeframe will be considered until the first datapoint for the following date has been published, after which any alterations will not be considered.

If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

markets.question: Map 1: Odd/Even Total Kills?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an even number.

"Total combined kills" includes all kills recorded during the match across all rounds, including kills during regulation rounds and overtime rounds if applicable. Team kills (friendly fire) and self-inflicted deaths (e.g., falling damage, own grenade) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

If no kills are recorded in Map 1, or if Map 1 is canceled (not played at all), or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Map 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

Trader consensus against impeachment before 2027 reflects Lee's consolidated position after a February 2026 court ruling convicted Yoon of insurrection and imposed a life sentence, which validated the prior removal process and reduced challenges to the current administration's legitimacy. With the Democratic Party holding a legislative majority, any impeachment motion would require a two-thirds National Assembly vote that remains structurally unlikely in the near term. Ongoing diplomatic engagements, including summits with the United States and regional partners, have further stabilized the executive amid economic and security priorities, with no major verified developments since early 2026 indicating imminent legislative or judicial action against the president.

markets.question: U.S. enacts AI safety bill before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if a bill that includes at least one of the following provisions is signed into federal law in the United States  by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. 

- Prohibition on Creation or Release: Forbids the creation or release of specific AI systems or models.

- Training Restrictions: Sets limits on how AI systems can be trained, such as restricting access to previously available training data or imposing a maximum limit on the number of parameters used for training.

- Usage Restrictions:  Prevents AI systems from being used in certain applications, such as interacting with customers, interfacing with other applications, or performing actions on the web.

- Human-in-the-Loop Requirements: Requires AI systems to include mechanisms ensuring human oversight or involvement in their operation.

Otherwise this market will resolve to "No". 

The resolution source will be official U.S. federal government (e.g., Congress.gov) however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Trump administration’s deregulatory approach, including the January 2025 revocation of prior AI safety executive orders and the December 2025 executive order establishing a “minimally burdensome” national framework with preemption of inconsistent state laws, has shaped congressional priorities toward innovation and federal uniformity rather than new safety mandates. Comprehensive AI safety legislation has seen limited advancement in the 119th Congress, with most proposals remaining in early stages or focused on targeted issues such as child safety, deepfakes, or voluntary standards instead of broad regulatory requirements. Ongoing state-level AI measures and recent White House legislative recommendations emphasizing regulatory sandboxes and preemption further signal that a federal safety bill faces structural and political hurdles through 2026. These developments underpin traders’ consensus favoring No.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Connecticut’s 1st congressional district maintains a pronounced Democratic advantage rooted in its urban and suburban voter base around Hartford, reflected in a Cook Partisan Voting Index of D+12 and consistent double-digit margins in recent cycles. Incumbent Democrat John Larson, seeking a 15th term, holds a commanding position ahead of the August 2026 primary against challengers including former Hartford Mayor Luke Bronin and State Representative Jillian Gilchrest, per early 2026 polling. All major forecasters rate the general election Solid or Safe Democratic, with Republicans lacking a competitive nominee or recent statewide breakthroughs in the state. Trader consensus pricing aligns with these structural factors and historical turnout patterns. A realistic shift would require an unanticipated primary upset or late-cycle national wave altering the district’s baseline.

markets.question: Will Trump speak to Maria Corina Machado in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed individual speaks with Donald Trump between May 1 and May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No".

Speaking is defined as any verbal interaction between the listed individual and Donald Trump, occurring either in person or through verbal communication by phone or video call.

The resolution source will be credible media reporting. However, in the absence of definitive or in the case of contradictory media reporting, statements by either of the specified individuals or their official representatives will also be considered.

Statements by the specified individuals or their official representatives will only be considered conclusive if the claims explicitly, or in context unambiguously, indicate that a qualifying talk occurred within the specified timeframe and took place via verbal communication, and provided that such claims are not contradicted by the other specified individual, their representatives, or credible media reporting by the end of the third calendar date (ET) following the statement in question.

If the date/time of a qualifying talk cannot be confirmed by a consensus of credible reporting by the end of the third calendar day following this market's above-specified timeframe, it will resolve to "No" regardless of whether it was later confirmed to have taken place.

markets.question: Will South Africa advance to the knockout stages at the 2026 FIFA World Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve “Yes” if the listed nation advances to the Knockout Stage of the 2026 FIFA World Cup. 

If at any point it becomes impossible for the listed nation to advance to the Knockout Stage (e.g. they are mathematically eliminated), the associated market will resolve to "No".

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup competition is cancelled, postponed after July 12, 2026, 11:59 PM ET or the 2026 FIFA World Cup Round of 32 matchups have not been declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from FIFA; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

markets.question: Will Mistral have the best Coding AI model at the end of May 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the company that owns the model that has the highest arena rank based on the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard (https://lmarena.ai/) when the table under the "Leaderboard" tab for "Coding" is checked on May 31, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

Results from the "Rank" column under the "Text Arena | Coding" Leaderboard tab at https://arena.ai/leaderboard/text/coding-no-style-control with style control off will be used to resolve this market.

Models will be ordered primarily by their leaderboard rank at the market’s check time. If two or more models are tied on rank, they will be ordered by their Arena score, including any underlying, unrounded, granular values reflected in the data below the leaderboard. If a tie still remains, alphabetical order of company names as listed in this market group will be used as a final tiebreaker (e.g., if the two models are tied by exact arena score, “Google” would be ranked ahead of “xAI”). This market will resolve based on the company that occupies first place under this ranking.

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the leaderboard comes back online and will resolve based on the first check after it becomes available. If it becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on another resolution source.

markets.question: Will Houston Texans win the 2027 NFL AFC Championship?

eventMetadata.context_description: Blake Miguez holds the lead in trader consensus for the Louisiana 5th Congressional District Republican primary due to his Trump endorsement, substantial fundraising edge exceeding $4 million in cash on hand, and prior service as state Senate majority leader. The field remains fragmented among state legislators Rick Edmonds and Michael Echols, Board of Regents Chair Misti Cordell, and lesser-known contenders including Austin Magee, with several candidates based outside current district lines.
